Description: Research Strategy: Type of research (Quantitative, Qualitative, or Mixed).
Data Production: How data were produced (Primary or Secondary).
Time Management: Historical or non-historical approach.
Space Management: Historical or non-historical approach.
Abstract
Research Questions: Presence of clear questions.
Working Hypotheses: Presence of guiding assumptions.
Research Hypotheses: Specific hypotheses to be tested.
Reference Time of Data: When the data refer to.
Data Collection Time: When the data were actually gathered.
Country/Countries of Reference: Where the study was conducted.
Geographical Coverage: Specific areas covered (e.g., regions).
Geographical Unit: Smallest units covered (e.g., cities or communities).
Population: The group the research refers to.
Observation Unit: Specific subsets or units of the sample (e.g., individuals or households).
Sample: The size of the sample.
Sampling Method: How the sample was selected (e.g., random).
Data Recording Method: How data were recorded (e.g., interview or survey).
Data Analysis Method: The technique used (e.g., ANOVA).
Recording Tools: Tools used for collection (e.g., interview guides).
Analysis Tools: Software used (eg SPSS).
